M.description: Total number of candidate hospital sites (including existing hospitals)

a.description: Coverage indicator: 1 if household i is within distance S of hospital j, 0 otherwise

p.description: Maximum number of new hospitals that can be opened

assumptions.description: Number of household clusters is positive.
Total number of candidate hospital sites is positive.
Population of each household cluster is non-negative.

code.python: assert all(v[i] >= 0 for i in range(nI))

assumptions.description: Maximum number of new hospitals is non-negative.
Number of existing hospitals does not exceed total hospital sites.

x.description: 1 if hospital j is opened, 0 otherwise

y.description: 1 if demand at household i is served by hospital j, 0 otherwise

constraints.description: All existing hospitals must remain open.
At most p new hospitals can be opened.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(gp.quicksum(x[j] for j in range(m, M)) <= p)

constraints.description: A hospital can only receive household assignments if it is open.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(y[i, j] for i in range(nI)) <= nI * x[j] for j in range(M))

constraints.description: Each household is assigned to at most one hospital.

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(gp.quicksum(y[i, j] for j in range(M)) <= 1 for i in range(nI))

constraints.description: Assignment is only permitted to hospitals within the maximum travel distance.
constraints.formulation: y_{ij} \leq a_{ij} \quad \forall i \in I,\; \forall j \in J

code.gurobipy: model.addConstrs(y[i, j] <= a[i][j] for i in range(nI) for j in range(M))

objective.description: Maximize the total population served by an assigned hospital.

code.gurobipy:
model.setObjective(
    gp.quicksum(v[i] * y[i, j] for i in range(nI) for j in range(M)),
    GRB.MAXIMIZE
)
